chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْقَوْلِ عِنْدَ الْإِصْبَاحِ وَ الْإِمْسَاءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 48 – The words (to be spoken) during the morning and the evening

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/284)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/284)
text: قُلْ أَسْتَعِيذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ وَ أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ أَنْ يَحْضُرُونِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
وَ قُلْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ يُحْيِي وَ يُمِيتُ وَ هُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْ‏ءٍ قَدِيرٌ

قَالَ فَقَالَ لَهُ رَجُلٌ مَفْرُوضٌ هُوَ قَالَ نَعَمْ مَفْرُوضٌ مَحْدُودٌ تَقُولُهُ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ وَ قَبْلَ الْغُرُوبِ عَشْرَ مَرَّاتٍ فَإِنْ فَاتَكَ شَيْ‏ءٌ فَاقْضِهِ مِنَ اللَّيْلِ وَ النَّهَارِ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Abu Jameela, from Muhammad Bin Marwan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Say, ‘I seek Refuge with Allah<sup>azwj</sup> from the Pelted Satan<sup>la</sup>, and I seek Refuge with Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, that he<sup>la</sup> would approach me, and that Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, He<sup>azwj</sup> is the Hearing, the Knowing’.

And say, ‘There is no god except Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, Alone, there being no associate for Him<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>. He<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Causes to live and He<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Causes to die, and He<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> is Able upon every thing’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘So a man said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Is it an Obligation?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, an Obligation, Limited. You should be saying before the emergence of the sun and before the setting, ten times. So if something is missed by you, so make up for it, from the night and the day’.
en.sarwar: 32. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from Muhammad ibn Ali from abu Jamilah from Muhammad ibn Marwan from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Say, ‘I seek protection with Allah against Satan, the condemned to be stoned. I seek protection with Allah against the approach of Satan. Allah is Hearing and Omniscient.’ Say, ‘No one deserves to be worshipped except Allah alone. Who has no partner. He gives life and causes death. He has power over all things.’ The narrator has said that a man than asked, ‘Is it obligatory?’ The Imam said, ‘Yes, it is obligatory and limited that you say before sunrise and sunset, ten times and if you miss it then make it up during the day or night. ’”
